skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gemini</name>
    <description>Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gemini/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>healthcheck</name>
    <description>Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>nano-banana-pro</name>
    <description>Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/nano-banana-pro/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>node-connect</name>
    <description>Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/node-connect/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-creator</name>
    <description>Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>tmux</name>
    <description>Remote-control tmux sessions for interactive CLIs by sending keystrokes and scraping pane output.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/tmux/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>weather</name>
    <description>Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/weather/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>kublai-task</name>
    <description>Build tasks optimized for Kurultai workflow with agent-aware routing and prompt optimization</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/skills/kublai-task/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>kublai-task-report</name>
    <description>Comprehensive task data collection and reporting for reflections and system improvement. Use when generating task completion reports, aggregating metrics for hourly reflections, analyzing error patterns, or tracking agent performance over time. Automatically collects token usage, execution timing, code changes, resource usage, quality signals, and context chain data.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/agents/main/skills/kublai-task-report/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>kurultai-router</name>
    <description>LLM-based task routing for the Kurultai multi-agent system</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/skills/kurultai-router/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>qmd-kurultai</name>
    <description>Local hybrid search over Kurultai agent workspaces, memory, and shared context using QMD</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/skills/qmd-kurultai/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>tolui-searxng</name>
    <description>Web research via SearXNG for Tolui verification and fact-checking tasks</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/skills/tolui-searxng/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>heartbeat-watchdog</name>
    <description>5-minute system health check (tick) for the Kurultai. Runs watchdog-gather.sh to collect Neo4j, Redis, cron, and agent health metrics. Use when checking system health, diagnosing failures, or understanding the tick pipeline.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/agents/main/skills/heartbeat-watchdog/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>kurultai-reflect</name>
    <description>Data-driven behavioral improvement loop for all Kurultai agents. Reads telemetry from task-ledger.jsonl (SKILL_INVOCATION, SKILL_OUTCOME, ACTION events), detects measured failure patterns, generates targeted WHEN/THEN rules, and produces structured skill improvement proposals. Replaces generic open-ended brainstorming with evidence-based diagnosis. Run hourly after meta_reflection.py.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/agents/main/skills/kurultai-reflect/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>myclaw-backup</name>
    <description>Backup and restore all OpenClaw configuration, agent memory, skills, and workspace data. Part of the MyClaw.ai (https://myclaw.ai) open skills ecosystem — the AI personal assistant platform that gives every user a full server with complete code control. Use when the user wants to create a snapshot of their OpenClaw instance, schedule periodic backups, restore from a backup, migrate to a new server, download a backup file locally, upload a backup file from another machine, or protect against data loss. Includes a built-in HTTP server for browser-based download/upload/restore without needing cloud storage. TRUST BOUNDARY: This skill archives and restores highly sensitive data including bot tokens, API keys, and channel credentials. Only install if you trust the operator. Always use --dry-run before restore. Never start the HTTP server without a --token.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/agents/main/skills/myclaw-backup/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>scrapling-research</name>
    <description>Web scraping and data extraction using Scrapling framework. Adaptive scraping that survives redesigns, Cloudflare bypass, concurrent crawling, MCP server integration. Use when agents need to scrape websites, monitor competitors, gather news, or extract structured data from web pages.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/agents/main/skills/scrapling-research/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>tock-gather</name>
    <description>30-minute agent telemetry collection (tock) for the Kurultai. Gathers task completion rates, queue depths, success rates, and system metrics across all 6 agents. Use when checking agent performance, understanding tock data, or diagnosing agent workload issues.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/agents/main/skills/tock-gather/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
</available_skills>

agent:main:main.lastHeartbeatText:
Your proposal approval handler is running at 5:56 AM.

This processes any Kurultai proposals that have achieved unanimous consensus, converting approved items into agent tasks for execution.

Use when the user wants to create a snapshot of their OpenClaw instance, schedule periodic backups, restore from a backup, migrate to a new server, download a backup file locally, upload a backup file from another machine, or protect against data loss. Includes a built-in HTTP server for browser-based download/upload/restore without needing cloud storage. TRUST BOUNDARY: This skill archives and restores highly sensitive data including bot tokens, API keys, and channel credentials. Only install if you trust the operator. Always use --dry-run before restore. Never start the HTTP server without a --token.

resolvedSkills.name: scrapling-research
resolvedSkills.description: Web scraping and data extraction using Scrapling framework. Adaptive scraping that survives redesigns, Cloudflare bypass, concurrent crawling, MCP server integration. Use when agents need to scrape websites, monitor competitors, gather news, or extract structured data from web pages.

resolvedSkills.name: tock-gather
resolvedSkills.description: 30-minute agent telemetry collection (tock) for the Kurultai. Gathers task completion rates, queue depths, success rates, and system metrics across all 6 agents. Use when checking agent performance, understanding tock data, or diagnosing agent workload issues.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gemini</name>
    <description>Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gemini/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>healthcheck</name>
    <description>Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>nano-banana-pro</name>
    <description>Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/nano-banana-pro/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>node-connect</name>
    <description>Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/node-connect/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-creator</name>
    <description>Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

Reads telemetry from task-ledger.jsonl (SKILL_INVOCATION, SKILL_OUTCOME, ACTION events), detects measured failure patterns, generates targeted WHEN/THEN rules, and produces structured skill improvement proposals. Replaces generic open-ended brainstorming with evidence-based diagnosis. Run hourly after meta_reflection.py.

resolvedSkills.name: myclaw-backup
resolvedSkills.description: Backup and restore all OpenClaw configuration, agent memory, skills, and workspace data. Part of the MyClaw.ai (https://myclaw.ai) open skills ecosystem — the AI personal assistant platform that gives every user a full server with complete code control. Use when the user wants to create a snapshot of their OpenClaw instance, schedule periodic backups, restore from a backup, migrate to a new server, download a backup file locally, upload a backup file from another machine, or protect against data loss. Includes a built-in HTTP server for browser-based download/upload/restore without needing cloud storage. TRUST BOUNDARY: This skill archives and restores highly sensitive data including bot tokens, API keys, and channel credentials. Only install if you trust the operator. Always use --dry-run before restore. Never start the HTTP server without a --token.

resolvedSkills.name: scrapling-research
resolvedSkills.description: Web scraping and data extraction using Scrapling framework. Adaptive scraping that survives redesigns, Cloudflare bypass, concurrent crawling, MCP server integration. Use when agents need to scrape websites, monitor competitors, gather news, or extract structured data from web pages.

resolvedSkills.name: tock-gather
resolvedSkills.description: 30-minute agent telemetry collection (tock) for the Kurultai. Gathers task completion rates, queue depths, success rates, and system metrics across all 6 agents. Use when checking agent performance, understanding tock data, or diagnosing agent workload issues.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gemini</name>
    <description>Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gemini/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>healthcheck</name>
    <description>Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>nano-banana-pro</name>
    <description>Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/nano-banana-pro/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>node-connect</name>
    <description>Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/node-connect/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-creator</name>
    <description>Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>tmux</name>
    <description>Remote-control tmux sessions for interactive CLIs by sending keystrokes and scraping pane output.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/tmux/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>weather</name>
    <description>Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/weather/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>kublai-task</name>
    <description>Build tasks optimized for Kurultai workflow with agent-aware routing and prompt optimization</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/skills/kublai-task/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <name>kublai-task-report</name>
    <description>Comprehensive task data collection and reporting for reflections and system improvement. Use when generating task completion reports, aggregating metrics for hourly reflections, analyzing error patterns, or tracking agent performance over time. Automatically collects token usage, execution timing, code changes, resource usage, quality signals, and context chain data.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/agents/main/skills/kublai-task-report/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

resolvedSkills.name: heartbeat-watchdog
resolvedSkills.description: 5-minute system health check (tick) for the Kurultai. Runs watchdog-gather.sh to collect Neo4j, Redis, cron, and agent health metrics. Use when checking system health, diagnosing failures, or understanding the tick pipeline.

resolvedSkills.name: kurultai-reflect
resolvedSkills.description: Data-driven behavioral improvement loop for all Kurultai agents. Reads telemetry from task-ledger.jsonl (SKILL_INVOCATION, SKILL_OUTCOME, ACTION events), detects measured failure patterns, generates targeted WHEN/THEN rules, and produces structured skill improvement proposals. Replaces generic open-ended brainstorming with evidence-based diagnosis. Run hourly after meta_reflection.py.

resolvedSkills.name: myclaw-backup
resolvedSkills.description: Backup and restore all OpenClaw configuration, agent memory, skills, and workspace data. Part of the MyClaw.ai (https://myclaw.ai) open skills ecosystem — the AI personal assistant platform that gives every user a full server with complete code control. Use when the user wants to create a snapshot of their OpenClaw instance, schedule periodic backups, restore from a backup, migrate to a new server, download a backup file locally, upload a backup file from another machine, or protect against data loss. Includes a built-in HTTP server for browser-based download/upload/restore without needing cloud storage. TRUST BOUNDARY: This skill archives and restores highly sensitive data including bot tokens, API keys, and channel credentials. Only install if you trust the operator. Always use --dry-run before restore. Never start the HTTP server without a --token.

resolvedSkills.name: scrapling-research
resolvedSkills.description: Web scraping and data extraction using Scrapling framework. Adaptive scraping that survives redesigns, Cloudflare bypass, concurrent crawling, MCP server integration. Use when agents need to scrape websites, monitor competitors, gather news, or extract structured data from web pages.

resolvedSkills.name: tock-gather
resolvedSkills.description: 30-minute agent telemetry collection (tock) for the Kurultai. Gathers task completion rates, queue depths, success rates, and system metrics across all 6 agents. Use when checking agent performance, understanding tock data, or diagnosing agent workload issues.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>

    <description>Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).</description>

resolvedSkills.name: kublai-task
resolvedSkills.description: Build tasks optimized for Kurultai workflow with agent-aware routing and prompt optimization

resolvedSkills.name: kublai-task-report
resolvedSkills.description: Comprehensive task data collection and reporting for reflections and system improvement. Use when generating task completion reports, aggregating metrics for hourly reflections, analyzing error patterns, or tracking agent performance over time. Automatically collects token usage, execution timing, code changes, resource usage, quality signals, and context chain data.

resolvedSkills.name: kurultai-router
resolvedSkills.description: LLM-based task routing for the Kurultai multi-agent system

resolvedSkills.name: qmd-kurultai
resolvedSkills.description: Local hybrid search over Kurultai agent workspaces, memory, and shared context using QMD

resolvedSkills.name: tolui-searxng
resolvedSkills.description: Web research via SearXNG for Tolui verification and fact-checking tasks

resolvedSkills.name: heartbeat-watchdog
resolvedSkills.description: 5-minute system health check (tick) for the Kurultai. Runs watchdog-gather.sh to collect Neo4j, Redis, cron, and agent health metrics. Use when checking system health, diagnosing failures, or understanding the tick pipeline.

resolvedSkills.name: kurultai-reflect
resolvedSkills.description: Data-driven behavioral improvement loop for all Kurultai agents. Reads telemetry from task-ledger.jsonl (SKILL_INVOCATION, SKILL_OUTCOME, ACTION events), detects measured failure patterns, generates targeted WHEN/THEN rules, and produces structured skill improvement proposals. Replaces generic open-ended brainstorming with evidence-based diagnosis. Run hourly after meta_reflection.py.

resolvedSkills.name: myclaw-backup
resolvedSkills.description: Backup and restore all OpenClaw configuration, agent memory, skills, and workspace data. Part of the MyClaw.ai (https://myclaw.ai) open skills ecosystem — the AI personal assistant platform that gives every user a full server with complete code control. Use when the user wants to create a snapshot of their OpenClaw instance, schedule periodic backups, restore from a backup, migrate to a new server, download a backup file locally, upload a backup file from another machine, or protect against data loss. Includes a built-in HTTP server for browser-based download/upload/restore without needing cloud storage. TRUST BOUNDARY: This skill archives and restores highly sensitive data including bot tokens, API keys, and channel credentials. Only install if you trust the operator. Always use --dry-run before restore. Never start the HTTP server without a --token.

resolvedSkills.name: scrapling-research
resolvedSkills.description: Web scraping and data extraction using Scrapling framework. Adaptive scraping that survives redesigns, Cloudflare bypass, concurrent crawling, MCP server integration. Use when agents need to scrape websites, monitor competitors, gather news, or extract structured data from web pages.

resolvedSkills.name: tock-gather
resolvedSkills.description: 30-minute agent telemetry collection (tock) for the Kurultai. Gathers task completion rates, queue depths, success rates, and system metrics across all 6 agents. Use when checking agent performance, understanding tock data, or diagnosing agent workload issues.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/eightctl/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>gemini</name>
    <description>Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/gemini/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>healthcheck</name>
    <description>Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>nano-banana-pro</name>
    <description>Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/nano-banana-pro/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>node-connect</name>
    <description>Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/node-connect/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-creator</name>
    <description>Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>

Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: tmux
resolvedSkills.description: Remote-control tmux sessions for interactive CLIs by sending keystrokes and scraping pane output.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: kublai-task
resolvedSkills.description: Build tasks optimized for Kurultai workflow with agent-aware routing and prompt optimization

resolvedSkills.name: kublai-task-report
resolvedSkills.description: Comprehensive task data collection and reporting for reflections and system improvement. Use when generating task completion reports, aggregating metrics for hourly reflections, analyzing error patterns, or tracking agent performance over time. Automatically collects token usage, execution timing, code changes, resource usage, quality signals, and context chain data.

resolvedSkills.name: kurultai-router
resolvedSkills.description: LLM-based task routing for the Kurultai multi-agent system

resolvedSkills.name: qmd-kurultai
resolvedSkills.description: Local hybrid search over Kurultai agent workspaces, memory, and shared context using QMD

resolvedSkills.name: tolui-searxng
resolvedSkills.description: Web research via SearXNG for Tolui verification and fact-checking tasks

resolvedSkills.name: heartbeat-watchdog
resolvedSkills.description: 5-minute system health check (tick) for the Kurultai. Runs watchdog-gather.sh to collect Neo4j, Redis, cron, and agent health metrics. Use when checking system health, diagnosing failures, or understanding the tick pipeline.

resolvedSkills.name: kurultai-reflect
resolvedSkills.description: Data-driven behavioral improvement loop for all Kurultai agents. Reads telemetry from task-ledger.jsonl (SKILL_INVOCATION, SKILL_OUTCOME, ACTION events), detects measured failure patterns, generates targeted WHEN/THEN rules, and produces structured skill improvement proposals. Replaces generic open-ended brainstorming with evidence-based diagnosis. Run hourly after meta_reflection.py.

resolvedSkills.name: myclaw-backup
resolvedSkills.description: Backup and restore all OpenClaw configuration, agent memory, skills, and workspace data. Part of the MyClaw.ai (https://myclaw.ai) open skills ecosystem — the AI personal assistant platform that gives every user a full server with complete code control. Use when the user wants to create a snapshot of their OpenClaw instance, schedule periodic backups, restore from a backup, migrate to a new server, download a backup file locally, upload a backup file from another machine, or protect against data loss. Includes a built-in HTTP server for browser-based download/upload/restore without needing cloud storage. TRUST BOUNDARY: This skill archives and restores highly sensitive data including bot tokens, API keys, and channel credentials. Only install if you trust the operator. Always use --dry-run before restore. Never start the HTTP server without a --token.

resolvedSkills.name: scrapling-research
resolvedSkills.description: Web scraping and data extraction using Scrapling framework. Adaptive scraping that survives redesigns, Cloudflare bypass, concurrent crawling, MCP server integration. Use when agents need to scrape websites, monitor competitors, gather news, or extract structured data from web pages.

resolvedSkills.name: tock-gather
resolvedSkills.description: 30-minute agent telemetry collection (tock) for the Kurultai. Gathers task completion rates, queue depths, success rates, and system metrics across all 6 agents. Use when checking agent performance, understanding tock data, or diagnosing agent workload issues.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>eightctl</name>
    <description>Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).</description>

Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: nano-banana-pro
resolvedSkills.description: Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: tmux
resolvedSkills.description: Remote-control tmux sessions for interactive CLIs by sending keystrokes and scraping pane output.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: kublai-task
resolvedSkills.description: Build tasks optimized for Kurultai workflow with agent-aware routing and prompt optimization

resolvedSkills.name: kublai-task-report
resolvedSkills.description: Comprehensive task data collection and reporting for reflections and system improvement. Use when generating task completion reports, aggregating metrics for hourly reflections, analyzing error patterns, or tracking agent performance over time. Automatically collects token usage, execution timing, code changes, resource usage, quality signals, and context chain data.

resolvedSkills.name: kurultai-router
resolvedSkills.description: LLM-based task routing for the Kurultai multi-agent system

resolvedSkills.name: qmd-kurultai
resolvedSkills.description: Local hybrid search over Kurultai agent workspaces, memory, and shared context using QMD

resolvedSkills.name: tolui-searxng
resolvedSkills.description: Web research via SearXNG for Tolui verification and fact-checking tasks

resolvedSkills.name: heartbeat-watchdog
resolvedSkills.description: 5-minute system health check (tick) for the Kurultai. Runs watchdog-gather.sh to collect Neo4j, Redis, cron, and agent health metrics. Use when checking system health, diagnosing failures, or understanding the tick pipeline.

resolvedSkills.name: kurultai-reflect
resolvedSkills.description: Data-driven behavioral improvement loop for all Kurultai agents. Reads telemetry from task-ledger.jsonl (SKILL_INVOCATION, SKILL_OUTCOME, ACTION events), detects measured failure patterns, generates targeted WHEN/THEN rules, and produces structured skill improvement proposals. Replaces generic open-ended brainstorming with evidence-based diagnosis. Run hourly after meta_reflection.py.

resolvedSkills.name: myclaw-backup
resolvedSkills.description: Backup and restore all OpenClaw configuration, agent memory, skills, and workspace data. Part of the MyClaw.ai (https://myclaw.ai) open skills ecosystem — the AI personal assistant platform that gives every user a full server with complete code control. Use when the user wants to create a snapshot of their OpenClaw instance, schedule periodic backups, restore from a backup, migrate to a new server, download a backup file locally, upload a backup file from another machine, or protect against data loss. Includes a built-in HTTP server for browser-based download/upload/restore without needing cloud storage. TRUST BOUNDARY: This skill archives and restores highly sensitive data including bot tokens, API keys, and channel credentials. Only install if you trust the operator. Always use --dry-run before restore. Never start the HTTP server without a --token.

resolvedSkills.name: scrapling-research
resolvedSkills.description: Web scraping and data extraction using Scrapling framework. Adaptive scraping that survives redesigns, Cloudflare bypass, concurrent crawling, MCP server integration. Use when agents need to scrape websites, monitor competitors, gather news, or extract structured data from web pages.

resolvedSkills.name: tock-gather
resolvedSkills.description: 30-minute agent telemetry collection (tock) for the Kurultai. Gathers task completion rates, queue depths, success rates, and system metrics across all 6 agents. Use when checking agent performance, understanding tock data, or diagnosing agent workload issues.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.</description>
    <location>/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

resolvedSkills.name: gemini

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck

resolvedSkills.name: nano-banana-pro

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: kublai-task

resolvedSkills.name: kublai-task-report

resolvedSkills.name: heartbeat-watchdog

resolvedSkills.name: kurultai-reflect
resolvedSkills.description: Data-driven behavioral improvement loop for all Kurultai agents. Reads telemetry from task-ledger.jsonl (SKILL_INVOCATION, SKILL_OUTCOME, ACTION events), detects measured failure patterns, generates targeted WHEN/THEN rules, and produces structured skill improvement proposals. Replaces generic open-ended brainstorming with evidence-based diagnosis. Run hourly after meta_reflection.py.

resolvedSkills.name: myclaw-backup
resolvedSkills.description: Backup and restore all OpenClaw configuration, agent memory, skills, and workspace data. Part of the MyClaw.ai (https://myclaw.ai) open skills ecosystem — the AI personal assistant platform that gives every user a full server with complete code control. Use when the user wants to create a snapshot of their OpenClaw instance, schedule periodic backups, restore from a backup, migrate to a new server, download a backup file locally, upload a backup file from another machine, or protect against data loss. Includes a built-in HTTP server for browser-based download/upload/restore without needing cloud storage. TRUST BOUNDARY: This skill archives and restores highly sensitive data including bot tokens, API keys, and channel credentials. Only install if you trust the operator. Always use --dry-run before restore. Never start the HTTP server without a --token.

resolvedSkills.name: scrapling-research
resolvedSkills.description: Web scraping and data extraction using Scrapling framework. Adaptive scraping that survives redesigns, Cloudflare bypass, concurrent crawling, MCP server integration. Use when agents need to scrape websites, monitor competitors, gather news, or extract structured data from web pages.

resolvedSkills.name: tock-gather
resolvedSkills.description: 30-minute agent telemetry collection (tock) for the Kurultai. Gathers task completion rates, queue depths, success rates, and system metrics across all 6 agents. Use when checking agent performance, understanding tock data, or diagnosing agent workload issues.

resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: nano-banana-pro
resolvedSkills.description: Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: tmux
resolvedSkills.description: Remote-control tmux sessions for interactive CLIs by sending keystrokes and scraping pane output.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: kublai-task
resolvedSkills.description: Build tasks optimized for Kurultai workflow with agent-aware routing and prompt optimization

resolvedSkills.name: kublai-task-report
resolvedSkills.description: Comprehensive task data collection and reporting for reflections and system improvement. Use when generating task completion reports, aggregating metrics for hourly reflections, analyzing error patterns, or tracking agent performance over time. Automatically collects token usage, execution timing, code changes, resource usage, quality signals, and context chain data.

resolvedSkills.name: kurultai-router
resolvedSkills.description: LLM-based task routing for the Kurultai multi-agent system

resolvedSkills.name: qmd-kurultai
resolvedSkills.description: Local hybrid search over Kurultai agent workspaces, memory, and shared context using QMD

resolvedSkills.name: tolui-searxng
resolvedSkills.description: Web research via SearXNG for Tolui verification and fact-checking tasks

resolvedSkills.name: heartbeat-watchdog
resolvedSkills.description: 5-minute system health check (tick) for the Kurultai. Runs watchdog-gather.sh to collect Neo4j, Redis, cron, and agent health metrics. Use when checking system health, diagnosing failures, or understanding the tick pipeline.

resolvedSkills.name: kurultai-reflect
resolvedSkills.description: Data-driven behavioral improvement loop for all Kurultai agents. Reads telemetry from task-ledger.jsonl (SKILL_INVOCATION, SKILL_OUTCOME, ACTION events), detects measured failure patterns, generates targeted WHEN/THEN rules, and produces structured skill improvement proposals. Replaces generic open-ended brainstorming with evidence-based diagnosis. Run hourly after meta_reflection.py.

resolvedSkills.name: myclaw-backup
resolvedSkills.description: Backup and restore all OpenClaw configuration, agent memory, skills, and workspace data. Part of the MyClaw.ai (https://myclaw.ai) open skills ecosystem — the AI personal assistant platform that gives every user a full server with complete code control. Use when the user wants to create a snapshot of their OpenClaw instance, schedule periodic backups, restore from a backup, migrate to a new server, download a backup file locally, upload a backup file from another machine, or protect against data loss. Includes a built-in HTTP server for browser-based download/upload/restore without needing cloud storage. TRUST BOUNDARY: This skill archives and restores highly sensitive data including bot tokens, API keys, and channel credentials. Only install if you trust the operator. Always use --dry-run before restore. Never start the HTTP server without a --token.

resolvedSkills.name: scrapling-research
resolvedSkills.description: Web scraping and data extraction using Scrapling framework. Adaptive scraping that survives redesigns, Cloudflare bypass, concurrent crawling, MCP server integration. Use when agents need to scrape websites, monitor competitors, gather news, or extract structured data from web pages.

resolvedSkills.name: tock-gather
resolvedSkills.description: 30-minute agent telemetry collection (tock) for the Kurultai. Gathers task completion rates, queue depths, success rates, and system metrics across all 6 agents. Use when checking agent performance, understanding tock data, or diagnosing agent workload issues.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: nano-banana-pro
resolvedSkills.description: Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: tmux
resolvedSkills.description: Remote-control tmux sessions for interactive CLIs by sending keystrokes and scraping pane output.

resolvedSkills.name: heartbeat-watchdog

resolvedSkills.name: kurultai-reflect
resolvedSkills.description: Data-driven behavioral improvement loop for all Kurultai agents. Reads telemetry from task-ledger.jsonl (SKILL_INVOCATION, SKILL_OUTCOME, ACTION events), detects measured failure patterns, generates targeted WHEN/THEN rules, and produces structured skill improvement proposals. Replaces generic open-ended brainstorming with evidence-based diagnosis. Run hourly after meta_reflection.py.

resolvedSkills.name: myclaw-backup
resolvedSkills.description: Backup and restore all OpenClaw configuration, agent memory, skills, and workspace data. Part of the MyClaw.ai (https://myclaw.ai) open skills ecosystem — the AI personal assistant platform that gives every user a full server with complete code control. Use when the user wants to create a snapshot of their OpenClaw instance, schedule periodic backups, restore from a backup, migrate to a new server, download a backup file locally, upload a backup file from another machine, or protect against data loss. Includes a built-in HTTP server for browser-based download/upload/restore without needing cloud storage. TRUST BOUNDARY: This skill archives and restores highly sensitive data including bot tokens, API keys, and channel credentials. Only install if you trust the operator. Always use --dry-run before restore. Never start the HTTP server without a --token.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: eightctl
resolvedSkills.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).

resolvedSkills.name: gemini
resolvedSkills.description: Gemini CLI for one-shot Q&A, summaries, and generation.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: nano-banana-pro
resolvedSkills.description: Generate or edit images via Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro).

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: tmux
resolvedSkills.description: Remote-control tmux sessions for interactive CLIs by sending keystrokes and scraping pane output.

resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: kublai-task-report

resolvedSkills.name: heartbeat-watchdog
resolvedSkills.description: 5-minute system health check (tick) for the Kurultai. Runs watchdog-gather.sh to collect Neo4j, Redis, cron, and agent health metrics. Use when checking system health, diagnosing failures, or understanding the tick pipeline.

resolvedSkills.name: kurultai-reflect
resolvedSkills.description: Data-driven behavioral improvement loop for all Kurultai agents. Reads telemetry from task-ledger.jsonl (SKILL_INVOCATION, SKILL_OUTCOME, ACTION events), detects measured failure patterns, generates targeted WHEN/THEN rules, and produces structured skill improvement proposals. Replaces generic open-ended brainstorming with evidence-based diagnosis. Run hourly after meta_reflection.py.

resolvedSkills.name: myclaw-backup
resolvedSkills.description: Backup and restore all OpenClaw configuration, agent memory, skills, and workspace data. Part of the MyClaw.ai (https://myclaw.ai) open skills ecosystem — the AI personal assistant platform that gives every user a full server with complete code control. Use when the user wants to create a snapshot of their OpenClaw instance, schedule periodic backups, restore from a backup, migrate to a new server, download a backup file locally, upload a backup file from another machine, or protect against data loss. Includes a built-in HTTP server for browser-based download/upload/restore without needing cloud storage. TRUST BOUNDARY: This skill archives and restores highly sensitive data including bot tokens, API keys, and channel credentials. Only install if you trust the operator. Always use --dry-run before restore. Never start the HTTP server without a --token.

Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator

Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: kurultai-reflect

Replaces generic open-ended brainstorming with evidence-based diagnosis. Run hourly after meta_reflection.py.

TRUST BOUNDARY: This skill archives and restores highly sensitive data including bot tokens, API keys, and channel credentials. Only install if you trust the operator. resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

  </skill>

    <location>~/.openclaw/agents/main/skills/kurultai-reflect/SKILL.md</location>

Use when the user wants to create a snapshot of their OpenClaw instance, schedule periodic backups, restore from a backup, migrate to a new server, download a backup file locally, upload a backup file from another machine, or protect against data loss. Includes a built-in HTTP server for browser-based download/upload/restore without needing cloud storage. TRUST BOUNDARY: This skill archives and restores highly sensitive data including bot tokens, API keys, and channel credentials. Only install if you trust the operator. resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator

Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

resolvedSkills.name: coding-agent
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.</description>
